6.14.2 Adult Apparel

‘+ Adult apparel must follow specific design restrictions when incorporating drawstring closures at the
hhood or neck area. Elasticized cords or ties may be of any free length, but use of plastic or metal tips are
prohibited when located near face, head or neck. I the hood or neck area has an elasticized cord with a
rigid tip, the cord can stretch and snap back, impacting the face and resulting in injuries. This prohibition
applies to alltypes of stretchy, elasticized, or bungee cords.

‘+ Permitted end finishing on elasticized cords includes any of the following; turned / stitched back ends,
knotted ends, heat set ends, unfinished ends, or other types of soft fabric tipping.

+ Looped elasticized cords with plastic or metal cord locks may be used, provided thatthe loop and cord
Jock ay lat and flush against the garment in the fully relaxed state. Ifthe cord lock is permanently
attached tothe garment, the looped elasticized cord may have some degree of slack.

+ Non-elasticized cords or ties of any free length may incorporate plastic or metal tips / ends. Examples of
‘non-elasticized cord materials include but are not limited to shoelaces, woven tapes, ribbons, ropes and.
‘other non-stretchy fabrications, et.

+ Refer to Figure 45 for examples of prohibited elasticized cords and TABLE 27 for examples of permitted
elasticized cords.
